message:
refactor(ssh): replace libssh-rs SshClient with native ssh subprocess for command execution

Replace the libssh-rs-based SshClient connect/execute/disconnect lifecycle
with a single unified execute_over_ssh_streaming() method that spawns the
native OpenSSH client as a subprocess. This eliminates the dependency on
the libssh-rs mux session management and fixes several operational issues:

**Why this change:**
- The SshClient wrapper required explicit connect/disconnect lifecycle
  management, and timed-out commands could leak SSH sessions if disconnect
  failed
- libssh-rs had issues with certain SSH configurations (ProxyJump, agent
  forwarding) that the native ssh binary handles natively
- The native ssh client inherits the user's ~/.ssh/config, known_hosts,
  and SSH agent automatically

**What changed in transfer.rs:**
- New execute_over_ssh_streaming() method that:
  - Spawns `ssh -o BatchMode=yes` with identity file, connect timeout,
    and ServerAliveInterval options
  - Passes the remote script via stdin to `sh -s` to avoid shell quoting
    issues with newlines and comments in the command payload
  - Reads stdout/stderr concurrently via tokio::sync::mpsc channels with
    spawned reader tasks for real-time streaming
  - Applies command_timeout via tokio::time::timeout, killing the local
    ssh process on timeout to terminate the remote command
  - Returns a CommandResult with exit code, accumulated stdout/stderr,
    and duration
- Both execute_on_worker() and execute_streaming() now delegate to
  execute_over_ssh_streaming() instead of using SshClient directly
- Added AsyncWriteExt import for stdin.write_all()

**Cargo.lock updates:**
- rich_rust bumped from 0.1.1 to 0.2.0 (adds backtrace, fancy-regex
  0.14, os_pipe, serde, smallvec, stdio-override dependencies)
- fancy-regex 0.14.0 added alongside existing 0.11.0 (used by
  termwiz via 0.11, rich_rust via 0.14)
- os_pipe 1.2.3 and stdio-override 0.2.0 added as new transitive deps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
